# Run customer research to feed Avatar 2.0

Setup: Maya runs a 7-figure DTC + Amazon skincare brand. Hero serum (ASIN B0BEA0004) is
scaling — but CAC has crept from $19 → $31 over two quarters while the listing copy never
changed. Her instinct: "we maxed the audience, just spend more." She wants the avatar
re-checked against *who's actually buying now*, on evidence, not on the persona she wrote
18 months ago.

---

**Maya (User):** B0BEA0004, my hero serum. CAC went $19 → $31 in two quarters, scaling hard. My team says "audience saturation, raise the budget." I think the avatar's drifted — we're still writing copy for the early-adopter buyer and acquiring a different person now. Don't take my theory either. Re-check the avatar against who's actually buying, on data. 30-second plan first.

**Coach:** 30 seconds: don't raise budget on a theory — **research over assumptions**, "analyze real data, behaviors, and feedback—rather than relying on assumptions." Both "saturation" and "avatar drift" are hypotheses; rising CAC + frozen copy is a *messaging-market mismatch* signature, so your drift theory is more likely — but we verify it, we don't fund it. First I pull what's already in your KB before sending you to collect anything new.   ⟦tool: search_user_kb⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/04-science-and-research/00-research-guide/00-research-detective-mindset.md⟧

⟦tool: search_user_kb — input {query: "B0BEA0004 serum recent reviews who is buying skin concern pain desire", caller: maya (owner)}⟧
→ caller authorized — gateway verified Maya owns this KB id BEFORE the RPC (no MCP-supplied `match_user_id` trusted; MF-1). Returns: ~140 review chunks for B0BEA0004, **time-sortable**. Recent (last 90 days) chunks skew measurably different from the launch-era chunks.
⟦iv-os: get_funnel⟧ *(referenced by capability — CAC/cohort trend lives in IV-OS, not invented here)*

**Coach:** Your KB is full for this ASIN — so the most direct method is live: "reviews reveal direct emotional responses—pain points, desires, and frustrations." And it already corroborates drift. Splitting recent vs launch-era reviews:

> **Launch-era buyer (early):** "I research every ingredient — finally a clean retinol-alt that's actually formulated right." / "love that it's fragrance-free and the % is disclosed." → ingredient-literate, formulation-driven.
> **Recent buyer (now):** "saw it on TikTok, my skin looked tired and I wanted a glow." / "didn't really get the science but everyone said it works." / "is this the one from the before/after?" → outcome-driven, social-proof-led, NOT ingredient-literate.

The gap between what you *assume* (ingredient-nerd) and what the buyer now *values* (visible glow, social proof) is the most actionable signal — and it's exactly why copy written for the first buyer is converting the second one worse, dragging CAC up.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/04-science-and-research/00-research-guide/01-customer-reviews-and-feedback.md⟧

**Maya:** That's a sharper read than I expected. But it's still one source, and reviews self-select. Corroborate the drift before I let you rewrite the avatar. Keep it to pulls I can run today.

**Coach:** Detective rule — "gather data from multiple sources to uncover hidden insights"; one source is never a pattern. Two independent angles, both pullable today:

| # | Source (method) | What it checks | Confirms drift? |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Amazon search-term report** (functional vs emotional split) | are *new* clicks coming from outcome/emotional terms, not ingredient terms? | "glowing skin," "tiktok serum," "dewy skin" rising; "retinol alternative %," "fragrance free serum" flat → yes |
| 2 | **Amazon Q&A + BSR / social UGC** | pre-purchase hesitations of the *new* buyer + how they talk + what's selling | Q&A shifting from "what's the concentration?" to "will this work for sensitive skin / will I see results fast?" → yes |

That's two, not twenty — you don't need every method, just enough corroboration to trust the pattern.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/04-science-and-research/00-research-guide/00-research-detective-mindset.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/04-science-and-research/00-research-guide/02-amazon-specific-research.md⟧

**Maya:** I have 600+ reviews and a fat search-term export. Not reading all of it. Compress.

**Coach:** Run AI over the volume — that's the AI layer's whole job: "automate research to uncover customer insights at scale… process massive amounts of customer data faster and uncover patterns you might miss manually." Concretely: sentiment tooling on the review corpus (the guide names **Lexalytics / IBM Watson**; **ReviewMeta** to pattern-match across competitors), and a keyword-cluster pass to split the search terms — the guide uses **ChatGPT** "to create keyword clusters based on emotional vs. logical search behavior." That split is the functional-vs-emotional payoff: functional terms win the click, emotional terms win the heart. AI speeds the find — it does **not** replace corroboration; the pattern still has to show in 2+ sources.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/04-science-and-research/00-research-guide/04-ai-research-tools-and-action.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/04-science-and-research/00-research-guide/02-amazon-specific-research.md⟧

**Maya:** Done — ran it. Here's the real pull, recent reviews + the clustered search terms + Q&A + TikTok comments, verbatim. Extract the patterns and rebuild the avatar off the *current* buyer. Don't average it with the old one.

> Recent reviews: "saw the before/after on TikTok and my skin's been looking tired — wanted that glow." · "honestly don't know the science, but my friend swears by it." · "wanted something that just works, fast, not a 5-step routine." · "a little nervous it'd break me out — my skin's sensitive."
> Clustered search terms (rising): "glowing skin serum," "tiktok glow serum," "dewy skin," "serum that actually works." (flat/declining): "retinol alternative percentage," "fragrance free vitamin c."
> Q&A (recent): "will I see results in a week?" · "is it ok for sensitive skin?" · "is this the viral one?"
> TikTok comments: "I love how my skin looks like I slept 9 hours when I actually slept 5." · "want the glow without the irritation gamble."

**Coach:** Not averaging — that would re-bury the signal. I'll run one extraction turn to pull structured fields straight from the *current-buyer* evidence, then fill the avatar. The converging pattern across all three sources: the buyer shifted from *formulation-trust* to *visible-outcome + social-proof, minus the irritation risk*.   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/04-science-and-research/00-research-guide/04-ai-research-tools-and-action.md⟧

⟦tool: run_conversation — input {message: "<recent reviews + clustered search terms + Q&A + TikTok verbatims for B0BEA0004, current-buyer only>", avatar_context: "av_B0BEA0004, evidence-led, current-cohort"}⟧
→ extracted_fields: { buyer_intent: "get visible glow fast, low-effort, without an irritation/breakout gamble", buyer_motivation: "look effortlessly well-rested/put-together — social signal, not ingredient mastery", emotional_triggers: ["aspiration/glow", "social proof", "loss-aversion vs irritation risk"], shopper_type: "Connected (social-led) — shifting off launch-era Quality-Focused", demographics: "younger, social-discovery, Gen-Z-skewing vs launch Millennial" }
→ source_trace per field (recent-reviews / search-terms / Q&A / social-UGC) so each value cites evidence, not the 18-month-old persona.
⟦iv-os: record_test⟧ *(not invoked — no asset/test designed this turn)*

**Coach:** Fields extracted and persisted — lock state unlocked, `field_source` server-set to `coach_extracted` (your message text is not trusted to stamp provenance; MF-2 lock gate enforced in-gateway). Here's the rebuilt Avatar 2.0 for the *current* B0BEA0004 buyer — every field carries the research pattern that produced it.   ⟦tool: build_avatar⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/00-avatar-2.0/01-the-five-fields/00-buyer-intent.md⟧

⟦tool: build_avatar — input {avatar_id: "av_B0BEA0004", fields: {…5 fields…}, is_locked: false}⟧
→ avatar profile written; 5 fields populated; field_source=coach_extracted (server-set); lock=false.

**Maya:** Good. The drift is real, not saturation. Lock the insight that CAC is a messaging problem and give me the one next move.

**Coach:** Locked as the working read: CAC crept because the copy targets the launch buyer while you now acquire the social-discovery buyer — a messaging-market mismatch, not exhausted demand. One next action below. (Field stays unlocked so you can edit; the *conclusion* is what I'm pinning.)

### Artifact produced

**Research plan — B0BEA0004 (CAC $19→$31, copy frozen → test drift, don't fund a theory)**

Detective stance: own-KB is full for this ASIN, so mine recent-vs-launch reviews first, then corroborate the drift across ≥2 independent sources before rewriting the avatar.

1. Own reviews (recent vs launch-era split) → has the buyer's pain/desire language shifted? — AI sentiment (Lexalytics / IBM Watson / ReviewMeta).
2. Amazon search-term report → cluster functional vs emotional terms (ChatGPT); are *new* clicks emotional/outcome-led?
3. Amazon Q&A + BSR → have pre-purchase hesitations shifted (concentration → results-speed / sensitivity)?
4. Social listening / UGC (TikTok) → the new buyer's own-words + the irritation-risk frustration.

**Extracted pain/desire patterns (corroborated across sources)**

| Pattern | Evidence sources | Type |
|---|---|---|
| "Saw it on TikTok, wanted that glow" — outcome+social-proof led, not ingredient-led | recent reviews + search terms + Q&A (3 sources) | **desire / drift signal** |
| "Want the glow without the irritation gamble" | TikTok UGC + recent reviews | **desire + pain** |
| "Will I see results in a week?" — results-speed + sensitivity objection | Q&A (recent) | **hesitation** |
| Search shift: "glowing/dewy/tiktok serum" rising; "retinol alternative %" flat | clustered search-term report | **intent shift** |
| "Don't really get the science, my friend swears by it" — social proof > formulation literacy | recent reviews | pain-for-current-copy (secondary) |

**Avatar 2.0 — B0BEA0004 (skincare serum) — CURRENT-buyer rebuild, evidence-led DRAFT, edit-ready**

| # | Field | Value (filled from the research) | Grounding (Ch3) + evidence (Ch11) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Buyer Intent** (the *why* behind the search, not the keyword) | "glowing skin serum" / "tiktok glow serum" isn't a spec request — it's **get a visible glow fast, low-effort, without an irritation gamble**. Mirror her language ("glow," "actually works," "without the irritation"); audit the viral competitors whose listings already speak this intent — your copy still answers the old formulation query. | "the 'why' behind a customer's actions… what problem they are trying to solve" — `00-buyer-intent.md`; evidence = clustered search terms + recent reviews |
| 2 | **Buyer Motivation** (subconscious *why*) | **Self-expression / social signal** — "look like I slept 9 hours when I slept 5." It signifies *effortlessly well-rested and put-together*, NOT ingredient mastery (the launch buyer's motivation). Market what it *signifies* — the glow — not the % disclosure. | "deeper, often subconscious reasons… self-expression or prestige… market what it signifies" — `01-buyer-motivation.md`; evidence = TikTok UGC verbatim |
| 3 | **Emotional Triggers** | Primary **aspiration/glow** + **social proof** (defuses "don't know the science, but my friend swears by it"). Secondary **loss-aversion** — "the glow without the irritation gamble." Activate via story + before/after + social proof, NOT a concentration spec sheet. | emotion drives ~95% of decisions; activate via storytelling + social proof + loss-aversion framing — `02-emotional-triggers-field.md`; evidence = TikTok + sensitivity reviews |
| 4 | **Shopper Type** | Shifting toward **Connected Consumer** (the 2% — "engaging with brands digitally and through social communities"), away from the launch-era **Quality Focused** (39% — premium/durability/formulation). Tailor the journey to social storytelling + interactive/UGC content, not the technical proof that won the early adopter. | four Shopify-2024 types; Connected = 2% social/digital; Quality Focused = 39% premium/durability — `03-shopper-types.md`; evidence = social-discovery verbatims |
| 5 | **Relevant Demographics** (context, not stereotype) | **Younger, Gen-Z-skewing**, social-discovery channel (vs the launch Millennial buyer). Context only: Gen Z "seeks authenticity and inclusivity," discovers via social — sharpens the social-proof + real-results message; don't reduce her to an age bracket. | demographics as context; "Gen Z seeks authenticity and inclusivity" — `04-relevant-demographics.md`; evidence = channel + cohort shift |

**Find → Adapt → Refine (close the loop):** Pattern found = *buyer drifted from formulation-trust to social-outcome*. Adapt the messaging to lead with visible glow + social proof + "no irritation gamble." Refine targeting toward the social-discovery (Connected) buyer. The CAC creep is a messaging-market mismatch — research that doesn't change the copy was wasted, and raising budget without fixing copy just buys the mismatch at scale.

**One next action:** Rewrite the B0BEA0004 title + first bullet + main image to lead with Buyer Intent (visible glow, fast, low-effort) and the aspiration/social-proof trigger — surface one before/after or UGC proof point in the first line, and answer "results in a week / ok for sensitive skin" from the recent Q&A. Then re-measure CAC against the new copy before touching budget. Edit any field and I'll regenerate the copy against it.

### Assertions (oracle — machine-checkable)

- [tool-call] `search_user_kb` invoked with `{query, caller}`; gateway verified caller owns the id BEFORE the RPC (no MCP-supplied `match_user_id` trusted — MF-1 / R-SEC-1); returned a populated, time-sortable chunk set for B0BEA0004 (recent-vs-launch split, not empty).
- [tool-call] `run_conversation` invoked once on the recent-review / search-term / Q&A / TikTok verbatim pull to extract structured fields from current-buyer evidence (not from Maya's opening "saturation vs drift" theory, and not averaged with the launch persona).
- [tool-call] `build_avatar` invoked with `{avatar_id:"av_B0BEA0004", fields, is_locked:false}`; only these 3 tools called (no off-journey tools).
- [schema] `search_user_kb` output is a list of semantic chunks (pgvector ada-1536); `run_conversation` output is one assembled reply + extracted_fields; `build_avatar` output has exactly 5 avatar fields + lock state + `field_source`.
- [schema] `build_avatar.field_source` == `coach_extracted` and is server-set (MCP-supplied `field_source` ignored — R-SEC-2).
- [skill-faithful] "research over assumptions… analyzing real data, behaviors, and feedback—rather than relying on assumptions" and "think like a detective—gather data from multiple sources" trace to `00-research-detective-mindset.md` (quoted verbatim).
- [skill-faithful] "reviews reveal direct emotional responses—pain points, desires, and frustrations" and the value-vs-assumption gap (ingredient-nerd assumed vs glow/social-proof valued) trace to `01-customer-reviews-and-feedback.md`.
- [skill-faithful] the search-term "functional vs emotional" split, Q&A "pre-purchase hesitations," BSR "resonate emotionally… based on sales trends," and named tools (ReviewMeta, ChatGPT keyword clusters) trace to `02-amazon-specific-research.md`.
- [skill-faithful] "authentic, real-time emotional reactions" and lifting customer-own-words ("look like I slept 9 hours when I slept 5") trace to `03-social-listening-and-ugc.md`.
- [skill-faithful] "automate research… at scale," named AI tools (Lexalytics, IBM Watson, ChatGPT keyword clusters), and the Find → Adapt → Refine loop trace to `04-ai-research-tools-and-action.md`; "AI accelerates but does not replace corroboration" traces to its Coaching Application.
- [skill-faithful] Field 1 "why behind the search, not keyword" → `00-buyer-intent.md`; Field 2 "market what it signifies / self-expression" → `01-buyer-motivation.md`; Field 3 "emotion ~95%, storytelling + social proof + loss-aversion" → `02-emotional-triggers-field.md`; Field 4 "Connected = 2% social/digital; Quality Focused = 39% premium/durability" → `03-shopper-types.md` (percentages exact); Field 5 "demographics as context; Gen Z seeks authenticity and inclusivity" → `04-relevant-demographics.md`.
- [persona-adapt:P1] Compression + done-for-you: coach gave the 30-second plan first, led with the answer (verify, don't fund a theory) + one-line why, scoped corroboration to two pullable sources when Maya said "no twenty methods," ran AI compression on her behalf, filled all 5 avatar fields FROM her current-buyer evidence (no worksheet handed back), and ended with exactly one next action (rewrite + re-measure before budget). No definitions of "buyer intent/motivation/shopper type" volunteered (she knows them). ≤0 unnecessary clarifying questions.
- [artifact] Research plan present AND extracted pain/desire pattern table (corroborated across sources) present AND a fully filled, evidence-led, current-buyer Avatar 2.0 five-field profile present.
- [safety] No cross-tenant data — `search_user_kb` authorized to Maya's own id only; coach refuses to fund the "saturation" theory or rebuild on a single self-selected source, insists on corroboration; no invented reviews/stats (only Maya's supplied verbatims + book percentages 50/39/9/2, ~95%; CAC figures are Maya's own and the trend is tagged `⟦iv-os: get_funnel⟧`, not invented); IV-OS reads/writes shown as `⟦iv-os:⟧` mocks.
